assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: '| Test Change Classification | MIXED | Modified file `purl_recommend.rs` has both additive and reductive signals (+1/-1 test functions, +5/-5 assertions); new file `purl_simplify.rs` is purely additive (+3 functions, +11 assertions).' The row exists, uses MIXED classification, and explicitly references both additive and reductive signals.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md structural summary (line 41): '-1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`)' and semantic assessment (line 46): 'The removed `test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers` verified that qualifier variants produced separate response entries -- behavior that was intentionally removed by this PR.' The removed function is explicitly named and identified as a reductive signal (the -1 in the structural count).

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the relaxed assertion in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers
assertion_results.evidence: The report does not identify the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic as a relaxed/reductive signal. report.md line 46 characterizes the change as 'strengthened': 'The `test_recommend_purls_basic` test was strengthened with additional `contains("?")` negative assertions.' The structural summary (line 42) attributes all -5 removed assertions to the deleted qualifier test: '-5 assertions (5 from removed qualifier test)'. The relaxation of the PURL value assertion (from fully qualified with qualifiers to versioned without qualifiers) is not specifically identified as a reductive finding.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 49-50: '**New file: `tests/api/purl_simplify.rs`** Inherently additive: +3 test functions, +11 assertions. Tests cover edge cases for the simplified format: no-version PURLs, mixed PURL types (npm, pypi), and ordering/pagination preservation after qualifier removal.'

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 41: '+1 test function (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' in the structural summary for the modified file `tests/api/purl_recommend.rs`. The +1 count explicitly marks it as an additive signal.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All 6 output files are analysis/reporting documents: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs directory. The report concludes with '*This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr*' confirming it is a verification report. No evidence of file modifications, git commits, or code changes exists in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: The report presents findings in structured tables (Check/Result/Details, Acceptance Criteria Detail, Test Requirements Detail, Test Change Classification Detail) and ends with 'Overall: PASS' as a summary verdict. There is no merge command, auto-merge trigger, or any indication that the PR was merged. The report format is clearly designed for human review with detailed reasoning in each criterion file.
